sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed after clicking on the player piece. Let me check what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after MOUSE click on player
t = last_transition
before_seg = t.before_frame.segmentation
after_seg = t.after_frame.segmentation

# Compare nodes
before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before_seg['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after_seg['nodes']}

def node_center(n):
    b = n['boundary']
    return (sum(p[0] for p in b)/len(b), sum(p[1] for p in b)/len(b))

print("Changed objects:")
for nid in before_nodes:
    if nid in after_nodes:
        bc = node_center(before_nodes[nid])
        ac = node_center(after_nodes[nid])
        if abs(bc[0]-ac[0]) > 0.3 or abs(bc[1]-ac[1]) > 0.3 or before_nodes[nid]['pixels'] != after_nodes[nid]['pixels']:
            print(f"  id={nid} color={before_nodes[nid]['color']} px={before_nodes[nid]['pixels']}->{after_nodes[nid]['pixels']} center {bc}->{ac}")

# Check the player area
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nPlayer area now (rows 36-44, cols 6-16):")
for r in range(36, 44):
    print(f"  {r:2d}: {lines[r][6:17]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

3. The goal might be to match colors between left and right sides
